summary: Matched and linked the Ice Climbers Blizzard item unit by promoting src/melee/it/items/itclimbersblizzard.c from NonMatching to Matching, renaming anonymous/item-table callback symbols to itClimbersBlizzard-prefixed names, adding proper header declarations, defining the Blizzard ItemStateTable in the item source, and updating GALE01 symbols/scratches. The decomp.dev bot reported linked code +840 bytes and one new .data match for main/melee/it/items/itclimbersblizzard from 0.00% to 100.00%.
what_changed: Changed configure.py entry for melee/it/items/itclimbersblizzard.c from Object(NonMatching, ...) to Object(Matching, ...).
Renamed it_802C2144 to itClimbersBlizzard_Spawn, it_802C2248 to itClimbersBlizzard_802C2248, and it_802C2358 to itClimbersBlizzard_802C2358 in symbols, scratches, source, and header declarations.
Renamed Blizzard item callbacks from generic it_2725_Logic64_* names to item-specific itClimbersBlizzard_DmgDealt, Reflected, Clanked, HitShield, Absorbed, ShieldBounced, and EvtUnk.
Updated the ItemLogicTable entry in src/melee/it/it_2725.c to call the newly named itClimbersBlizzard_* callbacks.
Normalized capitalization from itClimbersblizzard_* to itClimbersBlizzard_* for UnkMotion0 Anim, Phys, and Coll symbols.
Added ItemStateTable it_803F76A8[] in itclimbersblizzard.c with Anim, Phys, and Coll callbacks, matching the unit's .data.
Reorganized itclimbersblizzard.c so spawn/setup/motion/collision/callback routines are named and ordered without the previous chain of no_inline wrapper helpers around it_802C2358.
Updated GALE01 sdata2 constants at 0x804DD1F0 through 0x804DD200 from global it_* object names to local @159, @174, @175, and @176 labels.
Included it/items/forward.h in itclimbersblizzard.c, likely to satisfy item forward declarations used by the matched source.
smart_moves: Moved from address-based and generic it_2725_Logic64 names to item-scoped itClimbersBlizzard names, making the ItemLogicTable and symbol map self-documenting.
Kept unknown behavior names where meaning was still unclear, such as itClimbersBlizzard_802C2248 and itClimbersBlizzard_802C2358, instead of inventing unsupported semantic names.

Preserved matching while removing artificial no_inline wrapper layers around the animation-state transition helper, suggesting the final function ordering/source structure made the workaround unnecessary.
Used a small static inline spawn_item_0z helper to express common SpawnItem initialization while keeping the matched spawn routine readable.
Marked compiler-emitted floating-point constants as local @ labels in symbols.txt, avoiding misleading global it_804DD* names for local constants.
decomp_lessons: When an item unit becomes matching, update configure.py, symbols.txt, scratches.txt, headers, and any logic-table callsites together; otherwise linkage and symbol names can drift.
Item callback names should generally be item-prefixed once ownership is known, even if the behavior suffix remains generic, because these callbacks are shared through global item logic tables.
For item .data matching, define the ItemStateTable in the owning item source with the exact callback references rather than leaving the unit data unmatched.
Local sdata2 constants should be represented as local @ labels when evidence indicates they are compiler-generated locals, not exported globals.
Do not over-name unknown helper functions: address-suffixed item-prefixed names are preferable to speculative names when behavior is not fully identified.
naming_conventions: Use the item prefix itClimbersBlizzard_ with capitalized character/item words, not itClimbersblizzard_.
Spawn routines can use the suffix _Spawn when their role is identified, as in itClimbersBlizzard_Spawn.
Item interaction callbacks use item-prefixed behavior suffixes: _DmgDealt, _Reflected, _Clanked, _HitShield, _Absorbed, _ShieldBounced, and _EvtUnk.
Unknown item-owned routines may remain address-suffixed under the item prefix, e.g. itClimbersBlizzard_802C2248 and itClimbersBlizzard_802C2358.
Compiler-local constants in symbols.txt may use local @NNN labels with scope:local rather than global it_804DD* names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Promoted the unit to Matching only after source, symbols, and data labels were aligned.
Defined ItemStateTable it_803F76A8 directly with the Blizzard UnkMotion0 callbacks to achieve the reported .data match.
Used a static inline SpawnItem initializer helper that zeroes velocity, sets prev_pos.z to 0.0f, assigns parent gobj fields, sets x44_flag.b0, and calls Item_80268B18.
Removed nested no_inline wrapper helpers around it_802C2358 and called itClimbersBlizzard_802C2358 directly from the velocity/setup routine.
Adjusted sdata2 symbol scope/name metadata for floats and doubles to local @ labels, which matters for object/data matching and symbol hygiene.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.

Automated decomp.dev report for GALE01 showed linked code 30.34% (+0.02%, +840 bytes), matched data 20.47% (+16 bytes), linked data 28.73% (+40 bytes), and one new .data match for main/melee/it/items/itclimbersblizzard from 0.00% to 100.00%.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename itClimbersBlizzard_802C2248 or itClimbersBlizzard_802C2358 to semantic names.
Search for other item units that still use generic it_2725_LogicXX callback names in ItemLogicTable.
Compare spawn_item_0z-style SpawnItem initialization patterns across other projectile item matches.
Look for similar sdata2 global-to-local @ label fixes in item matching PRs.
Check if itclimbersice.c later resolves its static forward declaration for itClimbersBlizzard_UnkMotion0_Coll.
